What Is a Fabric Storage Building?

Built Tough.
Priced Smart.

A fabric storage building — also called a tension fabric structure or coverall building — combines a hot-dip galvanized steel frame with a UV-resistant, industrial-grade fabric membrane.

They deliver the strength of a conventional building at 30–50% lower cost, with faster installation, lower foundation requirements, and clear-span interiors that traditional steel simply can't match.

  • 20–30 year building lifespan
  • Clear-span — zero interior columns
  • Engineered to AB, BC & SK building codes
  • Faster to build than conventional steel
  • Natural lighting, reduced energy costs
  • Relocatable & expandable as you grow

Applications

What Are Fabric
Buildings Used For?

Our engineered fabric storage buildings serve clients across every major Western Canadian industry — from municipalities to oilfield operators.

Salt & Sand Storage

Municipal-grade buildings engineered for corrosive salt and sand. Meets provincial highway maintenance standards with wide clear-span access for loaders.

Most Popular

Equipment Storage

Protect heavy equipment from Western Canada's winters. Clear-span interiors — no columns — with full drive-through access for the largest machines.

Alberta & BC

Agricultural Storage

Grain, hay, and fertilizer storage across the prairies. Cost-effective alternative to steel buildings — faster build, lower foundation requirements.

Saskatchewan & AB

Oil, Gas & Mining

Remote-site fabric buildings for the energy sector. Fast mobilization, extreme climate engineering, and designed for sites where traditional construction isn't practical.

Northern AB & BC

Industrial Warehouses

Large-format fabric warehouses for staging areas, laydown yards, and bulk storage. Scalable, relocatable, and expandable as your operation grows.

Commercial

Municipal Facilities

Vehicle maintenance, public works storage, and infrastructure buildings for municipalities across AB, BC and SK. Engineer-stamped drawings for permits included.

Municipal

The only fabric building contractor in Western Canada offering an ongoing service program. Annual inspections, preventive maintenance, and priority repairs — so your building performs for decades.

05 — No Surprise Costs
Fixed-Price Quotes

We assess your site, your soil, and your conditions before we quote. You get a complete, transparent price that covers everything — no add-ons, no surprises when the crew shows up.

Our crews mobilize quickly from multiple locations across Western Canada. Whether you're in Fort McMurray, Prince George, Lethbridge, or Saskatoon — we get there and get it done.

Our Process

Inquiry to
Move-In Ready

Four steps. One contractor. No coordination gaps. S&T manages every phase so you don't have to.

01
Free Quote & Site Assessment

We assess your site, soil conditions, usage needs, and timeline. We recommend the right building size, profile, and foundation type for your application and location.

02
Engineering & Permits

Our team coordinates with engineers to produce stamped drawings meeting your provincial building code. We manage the permit process so you can focus on your operation.

03
Foundation & Installation

S&T crews handle complete site preparation, foundation work, steel frame erection, and fabric installation — all in-house. No subcontractors, no delays, no excuses.

04
Final Walkthrough & Support

We do a full inspection to confirm everything meets spec and is ready for use. We remain available for ongoing maintenance, inspections, and future service needs.

Common Questions

Fabric Building
FAQ

Everything you need to know before you buy — answered by people who've installed hundreds of fabric buildings across Western Canada.

How much does a fabric storage building cost in Alberta or BC?

Fabric storage building costs vary based on size, application, foundation type, and site conditions. As a general rule, fabric buildings cost 30–50% less than comparable conventional steel buildings — and require significantly less foundation work. Contact S&T for a free, no-obligation quote specific to your site and requirements.

How long do fabric storage buildings last in Western Canada?

Properly engineered fabric buildings typically last 20–30 years in Western Canada's climate. The galvanized steel frame lasts indefinitely. The fabric cover generally has a 15–25 year lifespan depending on UV exposure and climate conditions. S&T's ongoing maintenance program catches issues early and maximizes building life.

Are fabric buildings engineered for Alberta and BC snow and wind loads?

Yes — every fabric building S&T installs is fully engineered to meet your province's specific building codes, including regional snow, wind, and seismic load requirements. We provide engineer-stamped drawings for your permit application as part of our standard service. Nothing is generic or off-the-shelf.

Do fabric buildings require a foundation?

Most permanent fabric buildings require some form of foundation — typically concrete piers, grade beams, or engineered ground anchors depending on soil conditions and building size. S&T handles complete foundation and site preparation as part of our turnkey service. We assess your site during the quote process and recommend the right foundation for your conditions.

How long does fabric building installation take?

Most fabric building installations are completed in days to a few weeks depending on size and site complexity — significantly faster than conventional construction. S&T's in-house crews mobilize quickly across AB, BC, and SK. Because we handle everything in-house with no subcontractors, there are no delays waiting on trades coordination.

Can a fabric building be relocated or expanded later?

Yes. Unlike conventional concrete or steel buildings, fabric buildings can be relocated or expanded as your operation grows. This makes them ideal for businesses with evolving site requirements, operations at multiple locations, or projects that require a temporary-to-permanent solution. S&T can also handle future recovers, repairs, and modifications.
